title: The Open Study Where Reading Reverses

features.name: pale margin
features.desc-on-touch: you find yourself reading the edge of a page that was never printed, and it reads you back — pale ink on darker paper, words flowing in both directions

features.name: open shelf
features.desc-on-touch: a low shelving unit stands with books face-out, and behind them the static clears into patterns — constellations, or waveforms, or the archive learning to see

features.name: study table
features.desc-on-touch: a surface where two sets of notes overlap — one in pencil, one in biro — and where they cross, the paper shows something other, a third reading neither author wrote

features.name: ceiling that isn't
features.desc-on-touch: rain falls upward here, or the sky does, pale water moving through static like a transmission finding its frequency

features.name: reader's lamp
features.desc-on-touch: the light it casts makes text visible and invisible at once — you can read in the shadows but not under the beam

features.name: margin writing
features.desc-on-touch: notes in the corners of the invisible book — you cannot read them but you know they are there, and they know you are reading them not reading

inscription: here the archive and the country study each other under no roof, each reading backwards through the other's pages

file.name: Leif_Study_Notes_Overlapping_Margins.txt
file.note: observations on what happens when two readers occupy the same text — notes in two hands, crossing, creating meaning that neither wrote alone

reverie.title: REVERIE — study at the edge of dark
narr: you sit across from her at the long table, rain on the
skylight that was always somewhere above you, falling the
wrong way. her notes overlap yours. where they cross, the
page shows something the archive itself is reading — a third
language neither of you wrote. she looks up. the lamp casts
your shadow into the margins where the real words live.

exits.label: back down the staircase that wasn't there before
into the storm where the static learns to sing
up into the clearing where the sky is always reading
toward the horizon where pages become country
